message.content: Adversaries may rent Virtual Private Servers (VPSs) to support their operations, leveraging the flexibility and anonymity provided by cloud service providers. This technique complicates attribution efforts, as the use of VPSs can obscure the physical origin of malicious activities. Adversaries can quickly provision, modify, and dismantle their infrastructure, making it ideal for various stages of their lifecycle, including Command and Control. High-reputation cloud providers lend a veneer of legitimacy, while some VPS services require minimal registration, enhancing anonymity. Notable groups like Axiom, Dragonfly, HAFNIUM, LAPSUS$, and TEMP.Veles have exploited VPS infrastructure for their campaigns. Mitigation strategies include monitoring for unusual VPS activity, implementing strict access controls, and employing threat intelligence to identify and block known malicious VPS providers.
